keyPoints: Measuring homogenization via TAFD and multi-metric drift (JS/KL/PSI/Hellinger/Wasserstein) within a single project cycle.
Deploy independent countermeasure arms (deliberate difficulty, diverse sampling, output variation prompts) and evaluate them with per-feature drift and TAFD metrics.
Emphasize per-feature and per-prompt analytics (feature-level JS/KL/PSI, embedding dispersion) to detect convergence across prompts.
Anchor evaluation in a task taxonomy with TAFD to ensure diversity is meaningfully task-relevant.
Use a time-resolved drift dashboard with moving baselines plus static baselines to aid interpretability and compare arms.
Prioritize reproducibility by documenting prompts, model versions, seeds, and evaluation criteria for cross-team replication.
